page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-30-lssd is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 30 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 15 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The instance is equipped with 120 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of two NVMe SSDs totaling 804 GB of capacity. No GPU resources are included in this configuration.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores ranking in the top 25%. Memory performance is also strong, as reflected in the Passmark memory score. However, a notable tradeoff exists in memory write speeds, where large-block uncached write bandwidth falls into the bottom 25%. Multi-core processing, database operations, and web serving throughput consistently perform in the average range. For specialized tasks, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds on small language models, though prompt processing and larger model inference remain average.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c3d-standard-30-lssd offers a balanced resource density, combining dedicated AMD EPYC compute with high-speed local NVMe storage. This makes it a viable option for workloads requiring fast local disk access without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances. Suitable workloads include database hosting, text processing, web serving, and general-purpose enterprise applications that benefit from local SSD caching.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-30-lssd is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 30 dedicated vCPUs and 120 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor. It includes 804 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across two drives, with no GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU and memory performance, alongside top-tier text generation speeds for small language models, though large-block memory write bandwidth is a relative bottleneck. This configuration is well-suited for database operations, text processing, and general-purpose enterprise workloads requiring fast local storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-30-lssd is a general-purpose server with 30 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 120 GB RAM, and 804 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, offering strong single-core and memory performance.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC compute and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 30-vCPU AMD EPYC 9B14 processor with dedicated allocation.
Equipped with 120 GB of RAM and 804 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
Delivers strong single-core CPU and memory benchmark performance.
Achieves top-tier text generation speeds for small language models.
Exhibits a performance tradeoff with lower large-block memory write bandwidth.
Optimized for database hosting, text processing, and general-purpose applications.
